title: Tips For Preventing Workplace Violence Ascension
source_filename: Tips For Preventing Workplace Violence Ascension.docx

qas.q: if associated notice early warning signs of workplace violence, it is their responsibility to immediately report the suspicious behaviors to their leaders

qas.a:
Law Enforcement - Become involved and provide further direction when the situation warrants an official report.
Leader - Support the associate, consider leadership rounds for those at risk, etc.
Security - Collaborate with clinical teams to develop a safety plan
Associate - Report workplace violence incidents to leader/supervisor

qas.q: workplace violence can take the form of bullying, hostility, intimidation, or use of physical force or weapons or power
in an active shooter situation, your priority is to protect
it's not always possible to predict when someone is going to become violent
in an active shooter situation, you should encourage others to leave, but don't delay

associates and leaders should be aware of warning signs

select all the potential workplace violence warning signs
qas.a:
unidentified person in the area
arguing increasingly
person conspicuously ignoring staff
coworker referring to weapons being accessible at work

qas.q: tips for preventing workplace violence include
qas.a:
trusting your instinct
eliminating potential weapons
promoting respect

qas.q:
scenario 1 - Sarah

How can Sarah de-escalate the situation?
qas.a:
Be non-judgemental
Speak with a calm voice
Respect personal space
